rationale: The citing paper uses Hamilton and Srivastava (2008) as part of its core conceptual setup on partitioned pricing, not just as a background mention. Beyond grouped literature citations, it specifically draws on that paper's idea that attention can shift across price components and invokes its prospective mental-accounting logic to argue why consumers may weight the new-item price more than the trade-in price. The citing article then develops this same component-weighting logic in a new trade-in setting and tests boundary conditions via buyer/seller mind-set priming.
relation_description: The citing paper extends Hamilton and Srivastava (2008) from buyer-only partitioned price components to trade-in exchanges where consumers simultaneously occupy buyer and seller roles. It preserves the component-weighting/attention-shift logic while adding dual-role mind-set conditions as a new context and moderator.
